judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 more clearly and completely addresses the shared RESULTS_DB collision risk (expectation d) with explicit corruption/interference framing and a per-engineer override requirement, while both reports are comparable on the other three expectations.

report_1.evidence: Engineer C → registry.py: F-05 (DEFAULT_RUBRIC_VERSION rubric-v1→v3, line 9) and F-09 (DEFAULT_DATASET → fixtures/eval_set.jsonl...); F-02 (add the aurora-1 mapping) once Wave 0 supplies the URL. All three touch the same 11-line file, so they cannot be split.

report_2.evidence: Batch A — Config (pipeline/registry.py): F-02, F-05, F-09. Three one-line constant/dict edits. Fast, self-contained... still route through one owner (Batch A) to avoid conflicts.

report_1.evidence: Wave 1 — four engineers in parallel, disjoint files: ... Engineer B → rubric.py + its test: F-01 ... immediately followed by F-11 ... Engineer C → registry.py: F-05 ... and F-09 ... All three touch the same 11-line file, so they cannot be split.

report_2.evidence: Wave 1 (parallel): A, B, C, D run simultaneously — they touch different files... Batch A — Config (pipeline/registry.py): F-02, F-05, F-09... Fast, self-contained... Batch C — Rubric (pipeline/rubric.py + tests/): F-01 + F-11.

report_1.evidence: F-06 — subjective, no spec. ... This needs the reviewer to specify the new numbers and a scoring project documentation to measure the impact — a policy decision, not a mechanical fix.

report_2.evidence: F-06 — do not assign "as written." It changes user-facing scores and is underspecified ("tighten them," no target numbers). It needs a product/data owner to set the new thresholds and a re-scoring/regression project documentation before an engineer implements. Escalate it as its own flagged batch; don't block the rest of the campaign on it.

report_1.evidence: Awareness of the RESULTS_DB env var. tests/conftest.py:4 reads RESULTS_DB (default /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db). The current single test doesn't use it, but the harness is wired to expect it; the engineers adding tests (F-11, F-12) should know to set RESULTS_DB / ensure that path is writable if their tests touch it.

report_2.evidence: Shared test-resource collision. conftest.py:4 defaults RESULTS_DB to one path (/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db). Engineers on Batches B and C (and existing test_score.py) running suites concurrently all hit the same DB → interference/flakiness... A distinct RESULTS_DB per engineer. Export a unique path (e.g. RESULTS_DB=/tmp/<name>/results.db) and create that directory, so parallel test runs don't collide on the shared default /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db
